title: using is function coloring — spec denies it but propagation is real
description: Every function that calls a using(allocator) function must itself declare using(allocator), or wrap the call in a with block. This is function coloring by another name. The spec says 'using is not an effect annotation. There is no effect system tracking which functions allocate.' — but this is semantic deflection. The propagation is real. A caller who adds allocation to a deep helper function must update every function in the call chain up to the with block. The difference from async coloring: the propagation is via lexical scope rather than return type, so the chain ends at the nearest enclosing with block. But the spec does not explain this boundary clearly.
